content:
# Exodus Chapter 39 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 110–112; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 82–83 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_039_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_039_page_002.jpg`, `EXO_039_page_003.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_039_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_039_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — PRIESTLY GARMENTS AND COMPLETION:** The Leningrad Codex presents Exodus 39 across pages 110–112, covering the fabrication of the priestly garments (vv. 1–31) and the presentation of the completed tabernacle work to Moses (vv. 32–43). This chapter is the fulfillment of the instructions given in Exodus 28 (garments) and the conclusion of the entire construction narrative begun in ch. 35. The refrain "as YHWH commanded Moses" (ka'asher tsivvah YHWH et-Mosheh) appears seven times in vv. 1–31 and then seven more times in vv. 32–43, totaling fourteen occurrences — a deliberate echo of the creation narrative's sevenfold structure. The Vaticanus LXX (pp. 82–83, heading CAP. XXXIX visible on p. 83) shows significant reordering: much of the priestly garment material that appears in MT 39 was already incorporated into LXX 36 (= MT 28's instructions placed into the construction narrative), so that LXX 39 contains primarily the completion and inspection material (corresponding roughly to MT 39:32–43) along with anointing and consecration material. The MT's verse-by-verse garment construction in ch. 39 thus has no direct sequential parallel in Vaticanus's ch. 39.

---

## The Woven Service-Garments (v. 1)

**1** And from the blue [u-min-ha-tekhelet] and the purple [ve-ha-argaman] and the scarlet yarn [ve-tola'at ha-shani] they made [asu] garments of service [bigdei-serad] for ministering [le-sharet] in the holy-place [ba-qodesh], and they made [va-ya'asu] the holy garments [et-bigdei ha-qodesh] that were for Aaron [asher le-Aharon], as [ka'asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh].

> **Key terms:** בִּגְדֵי שְׂרָד (bigdei serad) = "garments of woven-service" — serad occurs only in the tabernacle context; likely a knitted/plaited fabric for wrapping sacred objects. בִּגְדֵי הַקֹּדֶשׁ (bigdei ha-qodesh) = "garments of the holy-place." **LXX:** Vaticanus places the garment fabrication primarily in LXX 36; the service-garments (στολὰς λειτουργικάς) appear there.

---

## The Ephod (vv. 2–7)

**2** And he made [va-ya'as] the ephod [et-ha-efod] of gold [zahav] and blue [tekhelet] and purple [ve-argaman] and scarlet yarn [ve-tola'at shani] and twisted linen [ve-shesh moshzar].

> **Key terms:** אֵפוֹד (efod) = "ephod" — the outer vest-garment of the high priest; five materials listed. **LXX:** τὴν ἐπωμίδα ("the shoulder-piece").

**3** And they beat out [va-yeraqqu] the plates of [et-pakhei] the gold [ha-zahav] and he cut [ve-qitsets] threads [petilim] to work [la'asot] in the midst of [be-tokh] the blue [ha-tekhelet] and in the midst of [u-ve-tokh] the purple [ha-argaman] and in the midst of [u-ve-tokh] the scarlet yarn [tola'at ha-shani] and in the midst of [u-ve-tokh] the linen [ha-shesh] — a work of [ma'aseh] a designer [khoshev].

> **Key terms:** וַיְרַקְּעוּ (va-yeraqqu) = "and they beat/hammered out" — from רקע ("to stamp/spread thin"); gold leaf pounded into wire-thin threads. פְּתִילִם (petilim) = "threads/cords" — gold threads interwoven with the colored yarns. **LXX:** ἐτμήθη τὰ πέταλα ("the plates were cut") — matching the metalworking description.

**4** Shoulder-pieces [khetafot] they made [asu] for it [lo], joining [khoverot]; upon [al] its two ends [shnei qetsotav] it was joined [khubar].

> **Key terms:** כְּתֵפֹת (khetafot) = "shoulder-pieces" — the two straps connecting front and back panels. חֹבְרֹת (khoverot) = "joining/coupled" — feminine plural participle of חבר. **LXX:** συνεχούσας ("holding together").

**5** And the band of [ve-kheshev] his ephod [afuddato] that was on it [asher alav] — from it [mimmenu] it was [hu], according to its work [ke-ma'asehu] — gold [zahav] and blue [tekhelet] and purple [ve-argaman] and scarlet yarn [ve-tola'at shani] and twisted linen [ve-shesh moshzar], as [ka'asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh].

> **Key terms:** חֵשֶׁב (kheshev) = "band/girdle" — the woven waistband of the ephod, integral to the garment ("from it"). אֲפֻדָּתוֹ (afuddato) = "his ephod-fastening" — the ephod's own binding. **LXX:** matches; ὁ ζωστήρ ("the girdle").

**6** And they made [va-ya'asu] the onyx stones [et-avnei ha-shoham] surrounded [musambot] by settings of [mishbetsot] gold [zahav], engraved [mefuttakhot] with engravings of [pittukhei] a signet-ring [khotam], upon [al] the names of [shemot] the Sons of Israel [benei Yisra'el].

> **Key terms:** שֹׁהַם (shoham) = "onyx/lapis lazuli" — identification debated. מֻסַבֹּת (musambot) = "surrounded/enclosed" — the stones set within gold filigree frames. פִּתּוּחֵי חֹתָם (pittukhei khotam) = "engravings of a seal." **LXX:** σφραγῖδος ("of a seal") — λίθους σμαράγδου ("emerald stones") for shoham.

**7** And he set [va-yasem] them [otam] upon [al] the shoulder-pieces of [kitfot] the ephod [ha-efod] — stones of [avnei] memorial [zikkaron] for the Sons of Israel [li-vnei Yisra'el], as [ka'asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh].

> **Key terms:** אַבְנֵי זִכָּרוֹן (avnei zikkaron) = "stones of memorial/remembrance" — Aaron carries the tribes before YHWH. **LXX:** λίθοι μνημοσύνου ("stones of remembrance").

---

## The Breastpiece (vv. 8–21)

**8** And he made [va-ya'as] the breastpiece [et-ha-khoshen] — a work of [ma'aseh] a designer [khoshev], like the work of [ke-ma'aseh] the ephod [efod] — gold [zahav] and blue [tekhelet] and purple [ve-argaman] and scarlet yarn [ve-tola'at shani] and twisted linen [ve-shesh moshzar].

> **Key terms:** חֹשֶׁן (khoshen) = "breastpiece" — the pouch-like garment worn over the ephod on the chest. מַעֲשֵׂה חֹשֵׁב (ma'aseh khoshev) = "work of a designer" — the same high-skill weaving as the ephod. **LXX:** τὸ λογεῖον ("the oracle-piece").

**9** It was square [ravua]; doubled [kaful] they made [asu] the breastpiece [et-ha-khoshen] — a span [zeret] its length [orkho] and a span [ve-zeret] its width [rokhbo], doubled [kaful].

> **Key terms:** כָּפוּל (kaful) = "doubled/folded" — forming a pouch. זֶרֶת (zeret) = "span" — the distance from thumb to little finger spread, approximately 22 cm. **LXX:** διπλοῦν ("doubled") — σπιθαμῆς ("a span").

**10** And they filled [va-yemalle'u] in it [vo] four [arba'ah] rows [turim] of stone [even]: a row [tur] — a ruby [odem], a topaz [pitdah], and an emerald [u-vareketh] — the first row [ha-tur ha-ekhad].

> **Key terms:** אֹדֶם (odem) = "ruby/carnelian" — from אדם ("red"). פִּטְדָה (pitdah) = "topaz/chrysolite." בָּרֶקֶת (vareketh) = "emerald/beryl" — from ברק ("lightning/flash"). **LXX:** σάρδιον, τοπάζιον, σμάραγδος — different stone identifications in places.

**11** And the second row [ve-ha-tur ha-sheni] — a turquoise [nofekh], a sapphire [sappir], and a diamond [ve-yahalom].

> **Key terms:** נֹפֶךְ (nofekh) = "turquoise/carbuncle." סַפִּיר (sappir) = "sapphire/lapis lazuli." יָהֲלֹם (yahalom) = "diamond/jasper" — from הלם ("to strike"). **LXX:** ἄνθραξ, σάπφειρος, ἴασπις.

**12** And the third row [ve-ha-tur ha-shelishi] — a jacinth [leshem], an agate [shevo], and an amethyst [ve-akhlamah].

> **Key terms:** לֶשֶׁם (leshem) = "jacinth/opal." שְׁבוֹ (shevo) = "agate." אַחְלָמָה (akhlamah) = "amethyst." **LXX:** λιγύριον, ἀχάτης, ἀμέθυστος.

**13** And the fourth row [ve-ha-tur ha-revi'i] — a beryl [tarshish], an onyx [ve-shoham], and a jasper [ve-yashfeh]; surrounded [musambot] by settings of [mishbetsot] gold [zahav] in their fillings [be-millu'otam].

> **Key terms:** תַּרְשִׁישׁ (tarshish) = "beryl/chrysolite" — named after Tarshish. יָשְׁפֶה (yashfeh) = "jasper." מִלֻּאֹתָם (millu'otam) = "their fillings/settings" — from מלא ("to fill"); the stones set flush in their gold frames. **LXX:** χρυσόλιθος, βηρύλλιον, ὀνύχιον.

**14** And the stones [ve-ha-avanim] were upon [al] the names of [shemot] the Sons of Israel [benei Yisra'el] — twelve [shtem-esreh] upon [al] their names [shemotam]; engravings of [pittukhei] a signet-ring [khotam], each-man [ish] upon [al] his name [shemo], for the twelve [li-shnem asar] tribes [shevet].

> **Key terms:** אִישׁ עַל־שְׁמוֹ (ish al-shemo) = "each-man upon his name" — each stone engraved with one tribal name. שֵׁבֶט (shevet) = "tribe" — literally "rod/staff." **LXX:** ἐκ τῶν ὀνομάτων ("from the names") — δώδεκα φυλαῖς ("twelve tribes").

**15** And they made [va-ya'asu] upon [al] the breastpiece [ha-khoshen] chains [sharshot] of twisted work [gablut], a work of [ma'aseh] braiding [avot], pure gold [zahav tahor].

> **Key terms:** שַׁרְשֹׁת גַּבְלֻת (sharshot gablut) = "chains of twisting/braiding" — rope-like gold chains. עֲבֹת (avot) = "braided cords." **LXX:** κρωσσούς ("twisted chains").

**16** And they made [va-ya'asu] two [shnei] settings of [mishbetsot] gold [zahav] and two [u-shnei] rings of [tab'ot] gold [zahav], and they put [va-yittenu] the two [et-shnei] rings [ha-tabe'ot] upon [al] the two [shnei] ends of [qetsot] the breastpiece [ha-khoshen].

> **Key terms:** מִשְׁבְּצוֹת (mishbetsot) = "settings/filigree-frames." טַבָּעוֹת (tabe'ot) = "rings" — the attachment hardware for the breastpiece. **LXX:** ἀσπιδίσκας ("small shield-shaped settings").

**17** And they put [va-yittenu] the two [shnei] braided-chains of [avotot] gold [ha-zahav] upon [al] the two [shnei] rings [ha-tabe'ot] upon [al] the ends of [qetsot] the breastpiece [ha-khoshen].

> **Key terms:** עֲבֹתֹת (avotot) = "braided-chains/cords" — connecting breastpiece to shoulder-pieces above. **LXX:** matches.

**18** And the two [ve-et shnei] ends of [qetsot] the two [shnei] braided-chains [ha-avotot] they put [natenu] upon [al] the two [shnei] settings [ha-mishbetsot], and they put them [va-yittenu otam] upon [al] the shoulder-pieces of [kitfot] the ephod [ha-efod], toward [el-mul] its face [panav].

> **Key terms:** אֶל־מוּל פָּנָיו (el-mul panav) = "toward its face/front" — the chains attach at the front of the shoulder-pieces. **LXX:** matches.

**19** And they made [va-ya'asu] two [shnei] rings of [tabe'ot] gold [zahav], and they set [va-yasimu] them upon [al] the two [shnei] ends of [qetsot] the breastpiece [ha-khoshen], upon [al] its edge [sefato] that was toward [asher el-ever] the ephod [ha-efod] — inward [baytah].

> **Key terms:** בָּיְתָה (baytah) = "inward/houseward" — the lower rings are on the inner edge of the breastpiece, facing the ephod. **LXX:** τὸ μέρος τὸ ἐσώτερον ("the inner part").

**20** And they made [va-ya'asu] two [shnei] rings of [tabe'ot] gold [zahav], and they put them [va-yittenu otam] upon [al] the two [shnei] shoulder-pieces of [kitfot] the ephod [ha-efod] — from beneath [mi-lemattah] — from [mi-mul] its face [panav], close to [le-ummat] its joining [mekhbarto], above [mi-ma'al] the band of [le-kheshev] the ephod [ha-efod].

> **Key terms:** מֶחְבַּרְתּוֹ (mekhbarto) = "its joining/seam" — where the shoulder-piece meets the body of the ephod. **LXX:** κατὰ σύμβασιν ("at the junction").

**21** And they bound [va-yirkhesu] the breastpiece [et-ha-khoshen] from its rings [mi-tabe'otav] unto [el] the rings of [tabe'ot] the ephod [ha-efod] with a cord of [bi-ftil] blue [tekhelet], to be [lihyot] upon [al] the band of [kheshev] the ephod [ha-efod], and so that the breastpiece [ve-lo-yizzakh ha-khoshen] would not be displaced from [me-al] the ephod [ha-efod], as [ka'asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh].

> **Key terms:** וַיִּרְכְּסוּ (va-yirkhesu) = "and they bound/laced" — from רכס ("to bind/tie"); securing the lower rings together. וְלֹא־יִזַּח (ve-lo-yizzakh) = "and it would not be displaced/slip" — from זחח ("to move away"). **LXX:** ἵνα μὴ χαλᾶται ("so that it would not loosen").

---

## The Robe of the Ephod (vv. 22–26)

**22** And he made [va-ya'as] the robe of [et-me'il] the ephod [ha-efod] — a work of [ma'aseh] a weaver [oreg] — all of blue [kelil tekhelet].

> **Key terms:** מְעִיל (me'il) = "robe" — the long blue garment worn under the ephod. כְּלִיל תְּכֵלֶת (kelil tekhelet) = "entirely of blue" — the only garment of a single color. מַעֲשֵׂה אֹרֵג (ma'aseh oreg) = "work of a weaver" — plain weaving, not the designer's work. **LXX:** ὑακίνθινον ὅλον ("entirely of hyacinth-blue").

**23** And the opening of [u-fi] the robe [ha-me'il] in its midst [be-tokho], like the opening of [ke-fi] a coat-of-mail [takhera] — a lip [safah] for its opening [le-fiv] all around [saviv]; it would not [lo] be torn [yiqqarea].

> **Key terms:** תַּחְרָא (takhera) = "coat-of-mail/leather-armor" — the neckline is reinforced like armor. שָׂפָה (safah) = "lip/border" — a woven binding. לֹא יִקָּרֵעַ (lo yiqqarea) = "it would not be torn" — a reinforced, untearable collar. **LXX:** ᾤαν ("a hem/border") — ἵνα μὴ ῥαγῇ ("so that it would not tear").

**24** And they made [va-ya'asu] upon [al] the hems of [shulei] the robe [ha-me'il] pomegranates [rimmonei] of blue [tekhelet] and purple [ve-argaman] and scarlet yarn [ve-tola'at shani] — twisted [moshzar].

> **Key terms:** שׁוּלֵי (shulei) = "hems/skirts" — the lower hem. רִמּוֹנֵי (rimmonei) = "pomegranates" — woven fabric pomegranates alternating with bells. **LXX:** ῥοΐσκους ("little pomegranates").

**25** And they made [va-ya'asu] bells [pa'amonei] of pure gold [zahav tahor], and they put [va-yittenu] the bells [et-ha-pa'amonim] in the midst of [be-tokh] the pomegranates [ha-rimmonim], upon [al] the hems of [shulei] the robe [ha-me'il] — all around [saviv] — in the midst of [be-tokh] the pomegranates [ha-rimmonim]:

> **Key terms:** פַּעֲמוֹנֵי (pa'amonei) = "bells" — from פעם ("to strike/pulse"); small gold bells producing sound as Aaron moves. **LXX:** κώδωνας ("bells").

**26** a bell [pa'amon] and a pomegranate [ve-rimmon], a bell [pa'amon] and a pomegranate [ve-rimmon], upon [al] the hems of [shulei] the robe [ha-me'il] all around [saviv] — for ministering [le-sharet], as [ka'asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh].

> **Key terms:** The alternating pattern stated explicitly: bell-pomegranate-bell-pomegranate. לְשָׁרֵת (le-sharet) = "for ministering" — the sound announces the priest's service. **LXX:** matches.

---

## The Tunics and Turban (vv. 27–31)

**27** And they made [va-ya'asu] the tunics [et-ha-kottonot] of linen [shesh] — a work of [ma'aseh] a weaver [oreg] — for Aaron [le-Aharon] and for his sons [u-le-vanav].

> **Key terms:** כֻּתֹּנֶת (kottonet) = "tunic" — the basic undergarment of white linen for all priests, not just the high priest. **LXX:** χιτῶνας ("tunics").

**28** And the turban [ve-et ha-mitsnefet] of linen [shesh], and the headbands of [ve-et pa'arei] the caps [ha-migba'ot] of linen [shesh], and the undergarments of [ve-et mikhnsei] the linen [ha-bad] — twisted linen [shesh moshzar],

> **Key terms:** מִצְנֶפֶת (mitsnefet) = "turban" — the high priest's headwrap. מִגְבָּעוֹת (migba'ot) = "caps/headpieces" — for the ordinary priests. מִכְנְסֵי (mikhnsei) = "undergarments/breeches" — linen trousers for modesty. **LXX:** κίδαριν ("turban") — μιτρας ("miters").

**29** and the sash [ve-et-ha-avnet] of twisted linen [shesh moshzar] and blue [u-tekhelet] and purple [ve-argaman] and scarlet yarn [ve-tola'at shani] — a work of [ma'aseh] an embroiderer [roqem], as [ka'asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh].

> **Key terms:** אַבְנֵט (avnet) = "sash/girdle" — the embroidered waistband, multicolored unlike the plain linen garments. רֹקֵם (roqem) = "embroiderer" — needle-work technique. **LXX:** ζώνην ("sash/girdle").

**30** And they made [va-ya'asu] the plate of [et-tsits] the crown of [nezer] the holiness [ha-qodesh] — pure gold [zahav tahor] — and they wrote [va-yikhtovu] upon it [alav] a writing [mikhtav] of engravings of [pittukhei] a signet-ring [khotam]: "Holy [qodesh] to YHWH [la-YHWH]."

> **Key terms:** צִּיץ (tsits) = "plate/blossom" — the gold diadem on the high priest's forehead. נֵזֶר (nezer) = "crown/consecration" — marking separation to God. קֹדֶשׁ לַיהוָה (qodesh la-YHWH) = "Holy to YHWH" — the inscription declaring the priest's dedication. **LXX:** πέταλον ("leaf/plate") — ΑΓΙΑΣΜΑ ΚΥΡΙΟΥ ("Holiness of the Lord").

**31** And they put [va-yittenu] upon it [alav] a cord [petil] of blue [tekhelet] to set [latet] it upon [al] the turban [ha-mitsnefet] from above [mi-lema'lah], as [ka'asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh].

> **Key terms:** פְּתִיל תְּכֵלֶת (petil tekhelet) = "cord of blue" — fastening the gold plate to the turban. מִלְמָעְלָה (mi-lema'lah) = "from above" — positioned at the top-front of the turban. **LXX:** matches.

---

## The Work Completed and Brought to Moses (vv. 32–43)

**32** And all the work of [va-tekhel kol-avodat] the tabernacle of [mishkan] the tent of meeting [ohel mo'ed] was completed [va-tekhel]; and the Sons of Israel [benei Yisra'el] did [va-ya'asu] according to all [ke-khol] that [asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh] — so [ken] they did [asu].

> **Key terms:** וַתֵּכֶל (va-tekhel) = "and it was completed/finished" — from כלה ("to finish/complete"); echoing Genesis 2:1 (va-yekhullu). כֵּן עָשׂוּ (ken asu) = "so they did" — emphatic confirmation of obedience. **LXX:** συνετελέσθη ("was completed") — matching the completion formula.

**33** And they brought [va-yavi'u] the tabernacle [et-ha-mishkan] unto [el] Moses [Mosheh]: the tent [et-ha-ohel] and all its vessels [ve-et-kol-kelav] — its clasps [qerasav], its boards [qerashav], its bars [berikhav], and its pillars [ve-ammudav], and its bases [va-adanav],

> **Key terms:** The inventory-presentation begins. קְרָסָיו (qerasav) = "its clasps." קְרָשָׁיו (qerashav) = "its boards/frames." בְּרִיחָיו (berikhav) = "its bars." **LXX:** matches the list of structural components.

**34** and the covering of [ve-et mikhseh] the rams' skins [orot ha-eilim] dyed red [ha-me'oddamim], and the covering of [ve-et mikhseh] the takhash skins [orot ha-tekhashim], and the curtain of [ve-et parokhet] the screen [ha-masakh],

> **Key terms:** אֹרֹת הָאֵילִם הַמְאָדָּמִים (orot ha-eilim ha-me'oddamim) = "skins of the rams, the reddened-ones" — dyed-red ram leather. תְּחָשִׁים (tekhashim) = "takhash" — an unidentified animal; perhaps dugong or fine leather. **LXX:** δέρματα κριῶν ἠρυθροδανωμένα ("skins of rams dyed red") — δέρματα ὑακίνθινα ("hyacinth-colored skins") for takhash.

**35** the ark of [et-aron] the testimony [ha-edut] and its poles [ve-et-baddav] and the mercy-seat [ve-et-ha-kapporet],

> **Key terms:** כַּפֹּרֶת (kapporet) = "mercy-seat/atonement-cover" — from כפר ("to atone/cover"); the gold lid with cherubim. **LXX:** τὸ ἱλαστήριον ("the propitiatory/mercy-seat").

**36** the table [et-ha-shulkhan] and all its vessels [et-kol-kelav] and the bread of [ve-et lekhem] the Presence [ha-panim],

> **Key terms:** לֶחֶם הַפָּנִים (lekhem ha-panim) = "bread of the face/presence" — the twelve loaves set before YHWH. **LXX:** τοὺς ἄρτους τῆς προθέσεως ("the bread of the setting-forth").

**37** the pure [ha-tahor] lampstand [et-ha-menorah] and its lamps [neroteiha] — lamps of [nerot] the arrangement [ha-ma'arakhah] — and all its vessels [ve-et-kol-keleiha] and the oil of [ve-et shemen] the light [ha-ma'or],

> **Key terms:** מְנוֹרָה (menorah) = "lampstand." נֵרֹתֶיהָ (neroteiha) = "its lamps." הַמַּעֲרָכָה (ha-ma'arakhah) = "the arrangement/row" — the lamps set in order. **LXX:** τὴν λυχνίαν τὴν καθαράν ("the pure lampstand").

**38** and the altar of [ve-et mizbach] the gold [ha-zahav], and the anointing oil [ve-et shemen ha-mishkhah], and the incense of [ve-et qetoret] the spices [ha-sammim], and the screen [ve-et masakh] of the entrance of [petakh] the tent [ha-ohel],

> **Key terms:** מִזְבַּח הַזָּהָב (mizbach ha-zahav) = "the golden altar" — the incense altar. שֶׁמֶן הַמִּשְׁחָה (shemen ha-mishkhah) = "oil of the anointing." קְטֹרֶת הַסַּמִּים (qetoret ha-sammim) = "incense of the spices." **LXX:** τὸ θυσιαστήριον τὸ χρυσοῦν ("the golden altar").

**39** the bronze altar [et mizbach ha-nekhoshet] and the bronze grating [ve-et mikhbar ha-nekhoshet] that was for it [asher-lo], its poles [et-baddav] and all its vessels [ve-et-kol-kelav], the laver [et-ha-kiyyor] and its stand [ve-et-kanno],

> **Key terms:** The bronze furnishings of the outer court: altar with grating, poles, vessels, and the laver. **LXX:** matches.

**40** the hangings of [et qal'ei] the courtyard [he-khatser] — its pillars [et-ammudeiha] and its bases [ve-et-adaneiha] — and the screen [ve-et-ha-masakh] for the gate of [le-sha'ar] the courtyard [he-khatser], its cords [et-meitarav] and its pegs [vi-teidoteiha], and all the vessels of [ve-et kol-klei] the service of [avodat] the tabernacle [ha-mishkan] for the tent of meeting [le-ohel mo'ed],

> **Key terms:** מֵיתָרָיו (meitarav) = "its cords/ropes" — the guy-ropes securing the structure. יְתֵדֹתֶיהָ (yeteidoteiha) = "its pegs." **LXX:** matches the comprehensive inventory.

**41** the garments of service [et-bigdei ha-serad] for ministering [le-sharet] in the holy-place [ba-qodesh] — the holy garments [et-bigdei ha-qodesh] for Aaron [le-Aharon] the priest [ha-kohen], and the garments of [ve-et bigdei] his sons [banav] for acting-as-priests [le-khahen].

> **Key terms:** לְכַהֵן (le-khahen) = "for acting-as-priests" — Piel infinitive of כהן; to perform priestly duties. **LXX:** στολὰς τῆς λειτουργίας ("garments of the service").

**42** According to all [ke-khol] that [asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh], so [ken] the Sons of Israel [benei Yisra'el] did [asu] all [et-kol] the work [ha-avodah].

> **Key terms:** כֵּן...עָשׂוּ (ken...asu) = "so...they did" — the obedience refrain repeated from v. 32. **LXX:** matches.

**43** And Moses saw [va-yar Mosheh] all [et-kol] the work [ha-melakhah], and behold [ve-hinneh] they had done [asu] it [otah] — as [ka'asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah], so [ken] they had done [asu] — and Moses blessed [va-yevarekh otam Mosheh] them.

> **Key terms:** וַיַּרְא (va-yar) = "and he saw" — echoing Genesis 1:31 ("And God saw all that he had made"). וְהִנֵּה (ve-hinneh) = "and behold" — the particle of discovery/confirmation. וַיְבָרֶךְ אֹתָם מֹשֶׁה (va-yevarekh otam Mosheh) = "and Moses blessed them" — paralleling God's blessing of the seventh day (Gen 2:3). The creation-tabernacle parallel is now complete: God saw and blessed creation; Moses sees and blesses the tabernacle. **LXX:** καὶ εὐλόγησεν αὐτοὺς Μωυσῆς ("and Moses blessed them") — matches.

---

## Structural Notes

**Verse count:** 43 verses (MT). **Parashah:** Pekudei (continued from 38:21).

**Creation parallel:** The chapter's conclusion deliberately mirrors the creation account: (a) "all the work was completed" (v. 32) parallels "the heavens and the earth were completed" (Gen 2:1); (b) the sevenfold "as YHWH commanded Moses" echoes the sevenfold "and God said" of Genesis 1; (c) "Moses saw...and behold" (v. 43) parallels "God saw all that he had made, and behold" (Gen 1:31); (d) "Moses blessed them" (v. 43) parallels "God blessed the seventh day" (Gen 2:3). The tabernacle is a microcosm — the re-creation of sacred space on earth.

**The obedience refrain:** "As YHWH commanded Moses" occurs in vv. 1, 5, 7, 21, 26, 29, 31, 32, 42, 43 — saturating the chapter with the theme of exact compliance. Every garment, every stone, every bell is made precisely as commanded.

**MT-LXX divergence:** The Vaticanus LXX diverges significantly from the MT in chapter structure. Much of the priestly garment fabrication (MT 39:1–31) was placed earlier in LXX 36, integrated with the original garment instructions. LXX chapter 39 (visible on Vaticanus p. 83 under CAP. XXXIX) focuses on completion, assembly, and anointing material — corresponding more closely to MT 39:32–43 and parts of MT 40. The content is ultimately the same, but the arrangement reflects different editorial traditions about how to present the relationship between divine instruction and human execution.
